The fact that the loan duration is short, because longer loan terms increase the likelihood of credit risk, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [1]
The fact that there is no checking account, because the absence of a checking account may actually serve as a protective factor, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that all previous credits were paid back duly, because a record of duly paid credits typically functions as a risk-mitigating factor, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.
